object_anchor.preserved_role: 0x80-byte mirror object previously mapped as a locale/game bundle mirror populated from client +0x19A10, +0x19A30, +0x19A58, +0x19AD0, +0x19D58, and +0x19AF0.

evidence.observation: loads RDX = [RDI +0x19E90], proving the path consumes the mirror object directly from the client root.
reads qword [RDX +0x20], then qword [RAX +0x8], recovering the same game-variant descriptor path that earlier artifacts mapped from client +0x19A58 descriptor +0x8.
reads byte [RDX +0x7E] into R8B before the formatting call, showing the mirror object contributes a compact flag or mode byte in addition to pointer fields.

uses literal 0x140B69918 = 'totp' as one fixed label argument.
uses literal 0x140B69910 = 'login' as a second fixed label argument.
calls helper 0x140679500 twice with the same mirror-derived object fields but different fixed literals, showing a deliberate two-label emission pattern rather than an incidental string copy.
installs two more fixed literals, 0x140B69908 = 'atotp' and 0x140B69904 = 'dn', into the destination object at [R14 +0xC38/+0xC40] before continuing.
calls 0x14015C350, the already-mapped login-controller raw-child installer.
immediately follows with 0x140130270, the already-mapped helper that prepares the auth/display-name sink at +0xB70.

careful_inference.statement: client +0x19E90 is no longer just a passive locale/game mirror. On this path it behaves like a login-preparation template object that contributes a string field, the current game display-name pointer, and a mode byte into a fixed-label helper sequence rooted on 'totp' and 'login', before the flow installs 'atotp'/'dn' labels and refreshes the active login-controller children.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.locale_descriptor_table: Extends the earlier 0x19E90 mirror-object mapping by adding its first concrete read-side consumer family and proving the +0x20 raw pointer is live in later login-preparation code.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.js5_plus30_display_name_seed_and_hyphen_: Strengthens the broader bootstrap/login display-name model because the game-variant display-name pointer now appears in another downstream preparation path, not only the JS5 +0x30 seed path.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login_display_name_sink_and_play_gate: Links the 0x19E90 path directly to 0x140130270, the previously mapped display-name sink helper, so the mirror object now feeds a known auth/display-name publication stage.
